# Phase: Private Matrix Homeserver (Synapse + Postgres) on Colossus — Plan Only

Provision a tiny, private, non-federating Synapse homeserver on Colossus for CCE transport, reachable over Tailscale (HTTPS via Tailscale Serve), supporting boring password login for exactly two users (Kevin + bot) and one private room.
This phase writes docs and architecture decisions only; no containers are started.
Purpose and scope: “CCE transport only”, replace matrix.org due to auth/MAS/OIDC friction.
Explicit non-goals: no federation, no bridges, no Element Web, no TURN, no SSO, no MAS, no public registration after bootstrap.
Intended access: tailnet-only; CCE and Element use the same base URL.
State locations (human-inspectable, local dirs): Synapse config/data, Postgres data, secrets env file.
Operator runbook (exact commands; clearly labeled “do not run yet in this phase”).
The “operator clipboard” runbook: generate config, bring up, create users, create room, invite/join, then point CCE at it and run `--check-connection`.
Include the Docker Compose YAML as a documented plan (in a code block), not as an active `docker-compose.yml` file in this phase.

`docs/near-term-architecture.md`: add a short note that matrix.org auth/MAS/OIDC has blocked reliable bot lifecycle; private Synapse is the transport reliability plan (still preserving substrate invariants).
`docs/decision-log.md`: add a decision entry: “Adopt private non-federated Synapse for CCE transport reliability (replacing matrix.org).”
`README.md`: add one bullet/link under Operational boundaries or a small Infrastructure section pointing to `docs/private-matrix-homeserver.md`.
Repo `.gitignore` additions (in next implementation phase): ignore `infra/matrix/state/**` and `infra/matrix/env/.env`
`synapse` service: `matrixdotorg/synapse` image, binds Synapse client port on localhost (e.g.
`postgres` service: `postgres:16` image, mounts `infra/matrix/state/postgres:/var/lib/postgresql/data`, uses env vars from `infra/matrix/env/.env` (user/password/db).
No reverse proxy container in Compose (TLS handled by Tailscale Serve).
Synapse config settings documented (operator will generate then edit):

turns.answer:
That `cmdand dquote>` prompt is zsh telling you it thinks you started a command with an opening `"` but didn’t close it (so it’s waiting for the closing quote). That happens easily with long one-liners.

Use this instead (it’s a heredoc; no fragile quoting). Paste exactly, then type your password when prompted:

cd /Users/kevinmattice/cce && .venv/bin/python - <<'PY'
import json
import getpass
import urllib.request
import urllib.error

cfg = json.load(open("config/cce.json", "r", encoding="utf-8"))
hs = cfg["homeserver_url"].rstrip("/")
mxid = cfg["user_id"]
device_id = (cfg.get("device_id") or "").strip() or None

localpart = mxid
if mxid.startswith("@") and ":" in mxid:
    localpart = mxid[1:mxid.index(":")]

pw = getpass.getpass("Matrix password (input hidden): ")

url = hs + "/_matrix/client/v3/login"
body = {
    "type": "m.login.password",
    "identifier": {"type": "m.id.user", "user": localpart},
    "password": pw,
}
if device_id:
    body["device_id"] = device_id

data = json.dumps(body).encode("utf-8")
req = urllib.request.Request(
    url,
    data=data,
    method="POST",
    headers={"Content-Type": "application/json", "Accept": "application/json"},
)

try:
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
        raw = resp.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
    j = json.loads(raw)
    print("login ok: yes")
    print("user_id:", j.get("user_id"))
    print("device_id:", j.get("device_id"))
    print("refresh_token provided:", "yes" if isinstance(j.get("refresh_token"), str) else "no")
    print("expires_in_ms provided:", "yes" if isinstance(j.get("expires_in_ms"), int) else "no")
except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
    detail = e.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
    try:
        j = json.loads(detail)
    except Exception:
        j = {}
    print("login ok: no")
    print("status:", e.code)
    print("errcode:", j.get("errcode"))
    print("error:", j.get("error"))
PY

If you accidentally end up at `cmdand dquote>` again, press `Ctrl+C` to cancel and repaste the block.

Paste back only the printed lines (`login ok`, `status`, `errcode`, `error`).

You’re right. I should not have framed that as “probably mistyped.” Given you just created the account and confirmed the password, the better conclusion is:

```text
Matrix.org is rejecting the new account’s password through the legacy Matrix m.login.password API.
```

That is different from “the password is wrong.” The account password may be perfectly valid in Element/account.matrix.org while still not working for bot-style API login. Matrix.org is now on the Matrix Authentication Service/OIDC path, and although the public login flows still advertise `m.login.password`, we’re seeing the compatibility path reject the credential for both the old and new accounts.

What we have verified:

```text
@cce-bot-colossus:matrix.org
homeserver: https://matrix.org
m.login.password localpart: 403 M_FORBIDDEN Invalid username/password
m.login.password MXID: 403 M_FORBIDDEN Invalid username/password
legacy user field: 403 M_FORBIDDEN Invalid username/password
```

So the room ID, invite, `device_id`, and display name are not the current blocker. Login fails before CCE can check any room.

My recommended fix now: stop trying to make Matrix.org password login work for this bot. Use a bot account on a homeserver that supports plain Matrix `m.login.password`, or set up a small CCE-controlled homeserver for the bot. That is the smallest reliable architecture for “CCE owns its bot session lifecycle.”

Second-best option: implement an OIDC/login-token bootstrap path for Matrix.org, but that is more complex and likely still involves browser-mediated auth or token refresh behavior. It also moves us away from the simple “bot username/password” mode we were trying to build.

Immediate next step I’d take in the repo: update the auth docs/root-cause note to say Matrix.org password-login was live-tested and rejected for a fresh bot account, so `password_login` is supported by CCE but not currently viable with these Matrix.org accounts. Then we can choose the next implementation path deliberately rather than wrestling Element forever.

- Matrix spec login flows: https://spec.matrix.org/latest/client-server-api/
- Matrix.org auth/MAS background: https://matrix.org/blog/2023/09/better-auth/

import json
import getpass
import urllib.request
import urllib.error

cfg = json.load(open("config/cce.json", "r", encoding="utf-8"))
hs = cfg["homeserver_url"].rstrip("/")
mxid = cfg["user_id"]
device_id = (cfg.get("device_id") or "").strip() or None

localpart = mxid
if mxid.startswith("@") and ":" in mxid:
    localpart = mxid[1:mxid.index(":")]

pw = getpass.getpass("Matrix password (input hidden): ")

url = hs + "/_matrix/client/v3/login"
body = {
    "type": "m.login.password",
    "identifier": {"type": "m.id.user", "user": localpart},
    "password": pw,
}
if device_id:
    body["device_id"] = device_id

data = json.dumps(body).encode("utf-8")
req = urllib.request.Request(
    url,
    data=data,
    method="POST",
    headers={"Content-Type": "application/json", "Accept": "application/json"},
)

try:
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
        raw = resp.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
    j = json.loads(raw)
    print("login ok: yes")
    print("user_id:", j.get("user_id"))
    print("device_id:", j.get("device_id"))
    print("refresh_token provided:", "yes" if isinstance(j.get("refresh_token"), str) else "no")
    print("expires_in_ms provided:", "yes" if isinstance(j.get("expires_in_ms"), int) else "no")
except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
    detail = e.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
    try:
        j = json.loads(detail)
    except Exception:
        j = {}
    print("login ok: no")
    print("status:", e.code)
    print("errcode:", j.get("errcode"))
    print("error:", j.get("error"))

Immediate next step I’d take in the repo: update the auth docs/root-cause note to say Matrix.org password-login was live-tested and rejected for a fresh bot account, so `password_login` is supported by CCE but not currently viable with these Matrix.org accounts. Then we can choose the next implementation path deliberately rather than wrestling Element forever.

Sources I checked for context:
- Matrix spec login flows: https://spec.matrix.org/latest/client-server-api/
- Matrix.org auth/MAS background: https://matrix.org/blog/2023/09/better-auth/
